title: YUNZII AKKO CS Switch, 3 Pins for DIY MX Mechanical Keyboard, 45 pcs (Ocean Blue Switch)
features: 【About AKKO CS Switches】 AKKO Custom Series (CS) switches are the newly designed 3-pin switches for customers who have specific typing and gaming needs. Each package has 45 switches. Six types of switches are available and no matter which kind of typing feeling you want, we cover it all.

【Compatibility】AKKO CS switches are designed with a general MX structure so that they can fit most keycaps sets. The switch has 3 pins but also fit in all 3 pins and 5 pins keyboards. Based on the strong compatibility, you can easily change the switch and customize your mechanical keyboard.
【Built-in LED Slot 】The AKKO CS switches are equipped with a north-facing LED slot for LED modification. It is also compatible with SMD LED, provides more fun and options for keyboard enthusiasts.

【Durable Lifespan & Improved Performance】With a lifespan of approximately 50 million times of keystrokes, you are assured of high quality and a long lifespan. The CS switches feature a spiral spring design that can make the typing more comfortable.

【Ocean Blue Tactile Switch】The tactile bump of the Ocean Blue switch is at 0.55mm and it ends at 1.88mm, which gives long-continued feedback. Different from the blue and brown switch, the Ocean blue switch has a medium tactile position that can give you an excellent experience in typing and gaming.

reviews.title: Very good for the price
reviews.text: I'm not a mechanical keyboard connoisseur, but needed to replace the switches on my cheap Redragon keyboard to get rid of the loud clicking noise. The original switches were blue switch clones and the clicks will wake up the neighbors. These are cheap enough that I decided to give them a try. They are pretty good in terms of touch and sound, from my perspective and my use. Will definitely resort to these again in the future if I need to replace keyboard switches, especially for budget keyboards.

title: ASUS ROG Falchion Wireless 65% Mechanical Gaming Keyboard | 68 Keys, Aura Sync RGB, Extended Battery Life, Interactive Touch Panel, PBT Keycaps, Cherry MX Red Switches, Keyboard Cover Case
description: COMPACT, WITHOUT COMPROMISE ROG Falchion is a 65% form-factor wireless mechanical keyboard designed to change the way you play. It has an interactive touch panel and innovative two-way cover case, and it’s the first ROG keyboard with wireless Aura Sync RGB lighting. The keys feature durable ROG polybutylene terephthalate (PBT) doubleshot keycaps and German-made Cherry MX RGB mechanical switches for precise, tactile keystrokes. ROG Falchion utilizes a gaming-grade 2.4 GHz RF connection for 1 ms report rate and offers up to 450 hours of battery life on a single charge.
features: Compact 65% layout masterfully incorporates arrow and navigation keys, in a 60% keyboard frame
Interactive touch panel makes it easy to adjust and customize controls
Dual-mode connectivity via wired USB and 1 ms at 2.4 GHz RF, and up to 450 hours of battery life* for stable, long-lasting performance (*with RGB lighting off)
Durable and long-lasting ROG PBT doubleshot keycaps
Innovative keyboard cover case provides on-the-go protection

reviews.title: More updates than microsoft!
reviews.text:
First of all, its a DUMB keyboard, meaning, you cant save the profile TO THE KEYBOARD... For this price, you think they could have bout a 64k ram chip in it...

So this means, that every time I hit my KVM, it goes to default, then I have to open the software to get my color profile back and EVERY SINGLE TIME I OPEN IT... It has to update the software... EVERY FLIPPIN DAY!

I hate this keyboard! 3 stars are too many!

Seriously, ASUS, I love your gaming laptop, but your software is garbage... Hire better programmers or even better, let me move the color profile to the KEYBOARD, so I never have to load your crappy software ever again!

Then after this loading screen loads for 5 minutes, I end up having to restart my computer to get it work again...

Seriously, this keyboard needs more updates than M$ and you have to restart your machine to use it after... if you want a keyboard that sux less, see 2nd image...

Final Update: I just updated the software and now it wont even connect to the keyboard anymore... Im DONE! Just ordered a DROP ALT, it has build in memory, so I can program the color profile and its WRITTEN TO THE KEYBOARD, no more daily updates, and constant frustration to get the stupid software to work, just so I can keep the color profile I want to be ALL THE TIME...

Good hardware, keyboard is solid, software ruined the product!

reviews.title: The RGB lights will sleep and won't wake up... annoying
reviews.text:
I bought this keyboard both for work and play. I use it for work during the day, and play games during the night. It's been a couple of months since I got this keyboard, and I love it. I love the form factor, I love the feel typing with it. Excellent for gaming. But with all that said, there is one annoying thing that warrants it to get 3 stars... the RGB lighting.

I got this plugged in to my 43 inch Aorus gaming monitor's KVM. When I work, this keyboard controls my work laptop, and when I game it controls my gaming PC via KVM. The RGB works fine on both computers, but when the computers goes to sleep and/or the screen turns off, the RGB also turns off. It's fine when it turns on when I wake up the computers, but majority of the time the RGB lights won't come back on from sleep, and that is annoying. When I manually lock the desktop, the RGB lights on the keyboard turns off, but when I unlock the desktop, the RGB lights won't turn on also. I thought something may be wrong with the KVM, but I tested the KVM using my Keychron keyboards, and they work fine, which means nothing is wrong with the KVM of the monitor. In order for the lights to work again, I have to unplug and re-plug the USB cable. It does the same thing even when in wireless mode.

I honestly like this keyboard when the RGB lights are working, and right now just keep on checking if there are new firmware for this keyboard everyday hoping that the annoying sleep bug will be fixed. So, 3 stars for now.

reviews.title: Nice keyboard bad software
reviews.text: The armory crate software is the worst I’ve used in my life. The actual keyboard is fine.

reviews.title: best keyboard I’ve ever had
reviews.text: I love this keyboard, had no idea that the type of plastic material the keys are made of makes a huge difference in how it feels and how enjoyable it is to use. The mechanical switches are great too but I sill really like this keyboard for the texture of the keys. I have nothing negative to say hope to have this keyboard for the next 20 years.

reviews.title: x2 Review Update - Great Keyboard
reviews.text:
Works great. Anyone looking at this keyboard already knows what it is and what it can do. I've heard it is a little more expensive than other brands that are doing the same thing. I don't know or care. I wanted this one, this brand. It's been flawless since purchase. - older review

8/26/22 - Update
Original keyboard is still working flawless since purchase. This keyboard has been working so well that I actually ended up buying a second one! I jump between multi-devices daily. Running the USB-C (charging cable) to one device and placing the Bluetooth dongle in another allows you to easily switch between them. You could very well run a USB-C cable to all your devices using color coded cables with some cable management have a USB-C (fountain like) connected to your desk and rotate through the cables to match the desired device.

This keyboard runs flawless on every device I have associated it with. Zero issues in regard to connectivity: brands, operating systems, etc.

Have to keep in mind that the heart of this keyboard is gaming. Very fast and instant with zero noticeable latency & lag. Wired - Bluetooth you will not be able to tell the difference. Large hands might get a little cramped. I've learned to work through it. The pros outweigh the cons. The only cons I can think that others would consider as such, would be the size and price. But you can't really fault it for that and here is why. 1) It's a 60-65% keyboard and you knew this prior of purchase. You can't fault it for what it is and its targeted users. i.e. "Well, it's not as comfortable on the arthritis as a full-size split keyboard" - duh... 2) You get what you pay for. There are more expensive kb's as well as cheaper. Rog Falchion sits nestled in the lower mid-range tier on pricing. Blue Clicky are on sale for $99.99 and the Red Linear are $129.99. That is a very fair price considering what is packaged.

Rog software is not the best but isn't the worst either. I have had a lot more issues with my Logitech software in comparison to what has basically been a stress-free experience with Rog. The software is not technically required to use the kb. If you want to make customizations such as changing the kb color, etc. You will have to download it. There are stock functions (modes) built-in that may suffice enough for some users or turn the RGB completely off to utilize maximum battery life. ** Side note, I'm rarely ever in a need to charge it. I leave RGB on at all times. I've left it on overnight, etc. The physical need to charge in order for me to use it is so far and few in-between that it feels like a chargeless keyboard. If you use a USB-C to connect to another device the Keyboard will charge while doing so.

Mine has been pretty damn resilient too. I have unintentionally knocked/thrown this keyboard via airborne a few times from my working area onto vinyl flooring. Besides a few small scratches on the case, little harm was done each time.

So, what do we have here?

We have a mechanical keyboard with MX Cherries. One has Red Linear switches and the other Blue Clicky.

I prefer the Blue Clicky!!! This keyboard is oh, so satisfying.

Yes, they are loud - oh no! However, no one cares. You don't purchase mechanical keyboards while pretending to be an office ninja. That is why I prefer the Blue Clicky over the Red linear.

The Red linear knows it's loud but tries to be quiet while at a higher cost. It delivers what feels like a finger-press to the depths of the Mariana Trench with an awkward/muffled low-toned like sound to match the embarrassment it is hiding from.

The Blue Clicky fully embraces its true self while doing so at a lower cost. It delivers a more satisfying user experience with touch and sound. Each key press has an instant-mechanical-click. Letting you know, "Hey I am here!" Delivering the sound and feeling of reaching the finish line with each finger press.

Overall - It is a wonderful keyboard. The Falchion will never make you feel like you wish it had more abilities. It will only make you feel like you want more of its capabilities. - Hints me buying two

Regardless of your switch preference you can't go wrong with this purchase.

reviews.title: Great looks, less great feel
reviews.text: These are supposedly double shot ABS keycaps, and they are for sure double shot, but they have the smoothest texture I've ever felt on a keycap. Think painted ABS gaming keyboard keycaps, that smooth if not even smoother. They don't sound half bad. I think the stems could fit stabilizers a little better seeing as if I push a bit too hard on one side the other lifts up at times. I can't complain much further considering I paid $27 for these after a discount and you get what you pay for. Overall I'd say they're worth the money, but don't go expecting super high quality or anything.

reviews.title: What a Fantastic Bargain
reviews.text:
I don't normally write reviews, but I liked this set and decided to pull the trigger on a product that barely had any reviews. I bought this yesterday and installed the keys a day later. I put these keys on my Glorious Pro keyboard and am in love. Now my experience is limited to the Kinetic Labs Whale keycap set and a cheap knock off from the China Amazon that are in OEM height, so not a direct comparison. These keys have the same build quality and sound as the Kinetic Labs set at almost 1/3 of the price.

I'm not an expert but I did take a picture of the spacebars and it does appear that the blue version may be warped slightly or disfigured, I don't know. I took the picture incase it helped. Outside of that, to my untrained eye everything else looked perfect. Gorgeous set.

I liked the options this seller had in other keycap sets, so I used this set as a trial run and was not disappointed. I will be looking to order other colors in the future.

title: SNAP 75% Mechanical Keyboard Kit (White)
description: A stylish, 75% split The SNAP is a 75% split mechanical keyboard kit with support for 2 rotary encoders, 2 OLED displays, speaker, and RGB underglow. We took all the best parts of the NIBBLE and added some of the most popular requests: true split shift/space/backspace. The SNAP is configurable, programmable, and easily assembled. 100% through hole design The SNAP uses a Pro Micro MCU instead of a discrete micro-controller chip. It offers the fun and aesthetic of a kit without having to worry about soldering the difficult bits like USB connectors. All required parts diodes, caps, and ICs) are through-hole, meaning they're easy and forgiving to solder. underglow LEDs are surface-mount Multiple Layout Options Use the SNAP in split mode, or snap it whoa, get it?) together for a more traditional layout. Strong internal magnets hold the two sides together and magical spring-loaded pogo pin contacts mean there's no need for a TRRS cable when the two sides are attached. The SNAP supports many configurations and layouts: Split space/backspace, split shift, ISO enter, and more. Since it runs QMK firmware and uses VIA, it's easy to change keymaps, program macros, and customize functionality. RGB underglow ready 10x optional bottom-mounted SMT LEDs shine nicely through the acrylic support material. Included Top & bottom PCB Acrylic supports M2 standoffs & screws Diodes, ICs, decoupling caps, reset switch RGB underglow, speaker, magnets, and pogo pins Rotary encoder & color-matched knob TRRS cable and mini bumpons Not included 2x Bit-Cs or Pro Micro Compatible MCU MX-compatible switches & keycaps PCB-mount stabilizers NOTE: This is a kit which requires assembly and soldering! Bit-Cs, switches, keycaps, and other accessories are sold separately
features: The SNAP is a high-quality split 75% keyboard kit with tons of room for customization
Use the SNAP together, or separate! It snaps together for a traditional 75% layout or pulls apart for use as a split board
Built around a Pro Micro, rather than a discrete microcontroller. This saves assembly time, and also reduces the build difficulty
All required parts (diodes, caps, and ICs) are through-hole, meaning they're easy and forgiving to solder

RGB underglow ready: 10x optional bottom-mounted SMT LEDs shine nicely through the acrylic support material. RGB underglow is supported natively in QMK, allowing for easy programming and configuration

reviews.title: Not for the faint of heart
reviews.text:
This kit does take a lot of work so I would not recommend it as a first build, or for someone looking for a quick build.

It is also a fairly unique one of a kind kit. I don't know of other split 75% keyboards with pro micro support that also have great documentation, support and community. If you are willing to put in the time you will be rewarded with a unique keyboard, and the kit really is fun to put together for experienced builders.

I would knock down half star as I encountered some issues with the fitting of the acrylic plate, specifically in these areas:
1. acrylic plate fitting at the keyboard split edge, had to use an exacto knife
2. acrylic plate fitting with bottom screws of the PCB mount stabilizers, had to make the holes wider
3. snap magnets did not fit, gave up trying to fit these
4. components pin through the solder joints interfered with acrylic, this is expected and is easily fixable by trimming them down.

Apart from that everything worked as expected. Firmware worked on first try and I was easily able to get it working with VIA.

reviews.title: Nice keys, font size a little horsey
reviews.text:
Note: I chose the Grey/Beige set. This set (YMDK 96 84 ANSI ISO Key) went on just fine, and are a great frugal find.

I bought these for an relatively inexpensive TKL mechanical wireless keyboard (RK87G) because the OEM keycaps were set in a truly fugly and illegible typeface.

These YMDKs feel good, and have a slightly deeper "thunk" when they bottom out. The two-character keys are typeset vertically, as Underwood and Remington intended. Although the font size on most of the keys is a big, they are fine and give the keyboard a bit of a homemade retro look.

NOTE Specific to the RK87G: This YMDK set does not have a ScrlLk key. The right Shift and right CTRL on this set are a little too small, so I just left the old ones on. I also left the OEM ScrlLk, Page Up, and arrow keys on because they have symbols for controlling the RGB LEDs. If you use the RKs function keys for app and control shortcuts, you may want to leave those on as well.

May 30, 2021
I've had these for a little over six months now. Still a great value set the money. I installed them on a keyboard I use for hours on end nearly every day. The printing on the beige keys hasn't faded, but I've noticed that the grey keys with white printing are beginning to fade. I'm not taking any stars off because before I purchased these I had already read other reviewers who had the same experience.

These caps seems well made enough, with a rough texture that provides ample grip without having a sandpaper effect on your fingers. The colors are a fair bit less saturated than the images make them look. Slightly annoying but they're still quite nice.

Do note that this set does not come with a long shift or scroll lock cap. This isn't an issue as they'll be going on a 65% for me but if you're looking to put them on a full size board you may be disappointed.

reviews.title: ULTRA Smooth, ZERO scratch and SAVES your RGBs, what else do you want?
reviews.text: These are so smooth you wont even believe it until you put it up to your ear and will hear nothing going on on the inside of the switch. After trying the Oil Kings and being super impressed, I said fine lets try the CJ Dark Blue Lubed switch and see what the fuss is about and I am glad I did. It is the end game switch. Stop the search right here. These are dead smooth a 10 out of 10, OK are about 7-10 and the Gateron Red Pro are somewhere like 5-10. They are all hard to tell and distinguish between the smoothness but once you test them out to your ear/finger you will be able to tell. I don't believe you need to lube these because well, they come lubed and since they are so smooth I don't see a need. Also the benefit with this switch is that this CJ Dark Blue comes with a slot for the LED to shine through just fine, so you will save your RGB as I have and I am glad.

reviews.title: Some of the best keycaps under $100
reviews.text:
These keycaps are fantastic. The quality of material is noticeable right when you open the package, and the layout compatibility is uncomparable. For about $60 you get keycaps that work with the following layouts:

Full Size
1800
96%
75%
65%
60%
40% (non ortholinear)

The only layouts that aren't supported are ortholinear (though you might be able to make something work) and split (no split spacebar here). 60% with arrows (the 64-key) also is not supported as there is no 2u shift.

The colors are fantastic, the material feels very nice, and the cap profile is just incredibly comfortable to type on. In fact, it's so comfortable the only caps that I think are nicer are Drop's MT3 which cost double and don't come with as much layout variation without buying extra kits.

This specific set, Dracula's Castle, also comes with custom novelites and two different colors worth of compatibility (crimson and black) for a lot of the keys to make your board look exactly the way you want.

I'd highly recommend anything in ASA profile. The Cherry profile is good too, but not s comfortable for me to type on personally.

reviews.title: So amazing for the price
reviews.text: They looks so good for the price, Akko does it again!

reviews.title: Great quality double shot pbt keycaps.
Pretty Great Set with an Interesting Profile
reviews.text: This set is pretty nice, unlike most of Akko's keycap sets this set doesn't directly rip off color ways from existing GMK or EBPT sets, it comes close to something like SA Bliss but it adds black mods so it's not exactly the same.  The keycaps themselves are fairly nice and the font of the legends are a lot more consistent than their cherry profile sets. The packing is nice albeit a little frustrating as you pretty much need to use a keycap puller to remove the keycaps. The ASA profile is what I found the most interesting, if I had to compare it to anything I would actually compare it to OEM profile but slightly taller with rounded edges than something SA profile, The sculpting is a lot more derivative of OEM than SA in my opinion. The typing feel is pretty great it is very reminiscent of OEM profile so if you are used to OEM profile you will be right at home. The sound is more clacky than thocky at least in my NK65 with Gateron Cap Gold Switches. Overall the keycaps are nice and the ASA profile is a pleasant surprise coming from someone that uses both SA and OEM profile fairly regularly.

reviews.title: Good quality, Vivid colors, Nice sounding, Great value
reviews.text:
Akko Los Angeles 158-Key ASA Profile PBT Double-Shot Full Keycap Set for Mechanical Keyboards with Collection Box

I really love the profile of these keycaps. I have a hard time with whether to call this company a knock off company or not. They do have their own color combos and make combos that other companies do for a lot cheaper. Are color combos and fonts really proprietary? I'm not sure and eventually there will be no more combination of colors that one company will be able to claim as only their colors. I do think that Akko make really good keycaps and you get a lot for your money. So that's my two cents.

reviews.title: High Quality Caps - Adore the Theme
reviews.text: As a typist, these caps have a great feel to them. They are thick without being heavy. No wobble. Improve the sound of the board and switch. I purchased the Smoke Cloud theme, and the images are adorable for a weather enthusiast like me. This set also had plenty of extra keys to complete the 96% keyboards, which not a lot of sets include. I was leary of paying $40 for a set without trying them first, but I don't regret it - great purchase - fast shipping!

reviews.title: Looks great, sounds like quality
reviews.text: I'm a bit of a keycap junkie.  I love being able to swap keys out to give my keyboard a different look.  If you're looking at this, you likely already like the look and I think they look better in person.  The keycaps are easy to install and provide good sound.  No hollow cheapness to these!

reviews.title: Very thick keycaps
reviews.text:
What I like
I really like the quality of the prints on these keycaps.
Also the thickness of these keycaps are even past some doubleshot ones I have.

What I find questionable.
The character keys looked like they were a light green color, but they are bright white. I ended up liking them anyway, just was a bit surprised.

Overall they are a great set of keycaps, going to keep using them for sure.

They are gorgeous, sophisticated, and they FEEL better than any other pudding keycaps I've ever had. No wobble, no cheap plastic sound. They add to the sound of the board and typing experience. Superior to HyperX. I have them paired with my Kailh Box Whites and I couldn't be happier.

reviews.title: Solid

The colors are nice and shine through the keys so you can see which letter it is in the dark.  It gives off just the right amount of light.  They look slick together on a desk and operate well.  Great little set.

reviews.title: Nice keyboard!!
reviews.text:
Updated review.. The mouse quit working after about 12 hours of use. This is disappointing because when it worked it was nice and responsive and was comfortable. When I left the original review everything worked fine..

This keyboard looks and works good. The back lighting makes it easy to see in the dark and the keys are precise and feel good while typing. The real aluminum frame is sturdy and looks good too. The phone holder is a nice touch. I would say it's worth it for the price.

Typing with Cherry MX Red switches is similar to browns, though it does lack tactile feedback and It is always the best preference for gamers.

reviews.title: Great
reviews.text:
It meets all my needs. I got it to replace a wireless Velocifire keyboard whose internal battery was dying. I had to plug my old one in everyday as I couldn’t see the key labels without the backlight, and it’s built in battery life decreased from 5 to 1 days over a couple years. So it was essentially wired, while I prefer the wireless aesthetic.

This one avoids the backlight killing the battery life as well as internal battery degradation. I can see the key labels fine without a backlight, and it takes replaceable batteries. Battery life should be months without the backlight, and it even has a low battery warning light. No cord to deal with.

Seems well built, heavy, and doesn’t slide on the desk. Nice price point. Easy to set up. It even has multiple tilt angle (height) options, although I like it flat. TKL is great to minimize the distance to reach the mouse, improving ergonomics.

I went from brown to red switches as that’s all that was in stock, and it was an easy change, although I could tell a slight difference initially.

reviews.title: Quite Loud
reviews.text:
If you can't stand the sound of a clacking keyboard, this isn't for you. It can get quite loud, especially when you're typing fairly quickly. This is probably better for techies who like the intense sound and the tactile key interface. The lighting is also nice. I can recommend this keyboard for those who either live or work alone.

Thanks so much for taking the time to read this review. I hope the information that I've provided helps make your decision a little easier.

reviews.title: A No-Cable Classic For Writers
reviews.text:
I am not lying: This is hands down the best keyboard for writing I have used in the last twenty years. I liked the older acrylic Apple keyboard and I own a mechanical keyboard with click-switches (blue), but this one makes touch typing so much more comfortable.
I wasn’t sure if I would like the tactile keys without click, because I actually like the click. They are just very loud. Logitech’s K845 (with Cherry MX red) is a great keyboard as well, but louder and has a cable.
I wish this keyboard would be backlit though. Black keyboards in front of big screens need some sort of illumination. I got one of these USB LED lights that I plugged behind my monitor, that half-solves the problem by providing some backlight.
Still it’s awesome to write on it. It’s focus is to be a mechanical keyboard. So much that it seems that it skipped the product design. It looks like an OEM-Keyboard from 1995. That’s in a way uncompromisingly functional, but next to a modern flat butterfly keyboard it looks like someone parked an old Volvo next to a Miata.
The quality is good. The keycaps are heavy and lettering sharp. It might be confusing for some that the ‘upper’ characters are printed next to the main set in the same size.
Battery life seems not to be a problem. I am pretty much writing all day, and I’m still on the first set of batteries that came in the package.
PROS AND CONS
+ Precise keyboard for touch-typing
+ Quiet enough for shared work areas
+ Height adjustable
+ Great quality
+ Long Battery life
+ Connects via dongle, not Bluetooth (yes, for me that’s a pro. Bluetooth sucks)
+ Compact with no number pad

- Chunky design
- No backlight
- Very heavy
- Vague keycap lettering
- No number pad (*conflict*)
I can recommend this keyboard to anyone who is typing over a long amount of times. It takes a while to get used to, but once adjusted it’s less tiring than modern butterfly or rubber cap keyboards, because the keys don’t bottom out that easily, and feel better to the fingertips. I took a few photos. I hope it helps to make a decision.

reviews.title: Silent Cherry Red Switches Work Well With Family Room Computer
reviews.text:
While the cherry MX red keys are often used for gaming, I am reviewing this as a typing keyboard in a family room, where the loud clackity clack of a keyboard is unwelcome. And I find this keyboard to be a great solution for such an environment.

This keyboard paired so effortlessly with my Mac that I wasn’t even aware it happened. I checked the keys for their mapping, and found them to be as one would expect for a Windows keyboard—just the usual issue with the non-Apple standard keys near the Shift key.

It is not “full-size,” but 80% size, so it is somewhat smaller, but not so much so that you fee cramped.

They keys are as silent as advertised. And while an old-school touch typist might miss the “click” feedback of a mechanical keyboard, these keys have decent travel, so if you are typing, you will have little doubt that you have actually engaged the key. (I can’t talk to gaming with these.)

I have had no trouble with the bluetooth connection, nor the responsiveness while typing.

If I have any reliability issues, I will be sure to revisit this review as necessary.

reviews.title: Spacebar sticks occasionally.
reviews.text: The space bar sticks sometimes, but the actuation for is suitable for my sore index finger from using my mouse a lot. I am a smaller person, my hands fall between medium and small. I wanted something with a smaller key distance and slightly smaller keys for typing and this definitely fits the bill. It comes with some replacement switches, we'll see how long it takes to actually need to the switch.

reviews.title: I LOVE it!
reviews.text: Looks amazing in my set up and love the retro typewriter feel.

reviews.title: Hubby loves it!
reviews.text: So I initially got this for my parents, I mistakenly thought it was a wireless keyboard. IT IS NOT A WIRELESS KEYBOARD. However, I intended to take it back until my husband got a hold of it and loved the lights and the different patterns that can be done as well as the old fashioned typing clicks.

The only problem with the texture part is that the A,W,S,D buttons are going to wear down and it will smooth out. I would appreciate if they can do something to make it more durable. reviews.title: Light, snappy, like a punchier Gat brown
reviews.text:
I previously got a tactile switch sampler set and honestly loved the AJAZZ Banana more than U4 Boba and its other iterations. I liked these more than Glorious Panda and Holy Panda. I type a lot as a student, so I didn't want anything too heavy--it gets tiring. I've been using AJAZZ Banana for two days so far now and I LOVE it!

I was using lubed Gateron browns before. I wanted something punchier but not too heavy. Banana is the perfect answer to that. Actuation force is about the same to Gat browns. I have a north-facing RGB board and the lights come through Banana switches beautifully.

In answer to the other reviewer, I believe that the Banana switches ARE lubed (with what, I don't know). I have a stock/un-lubed AJAZZ Banana switch from my switch sampler pack, these pre-lubed ones feel slightly lighter in comparison. Out of the box, these feel great without additional lubing. Results will vary depending on your mech keeb setup.

Note that the switch pack comes with 46 switches, so it won't be enough to do a full board. I have a 60% board and had enough to do the alphanumeric keys and other frequently pressed keys like Shift, Enter, Ctrl, Backspace, Alt, Tab, and punctuation keys. I left my Gat browns on the less frequent keys. I have two "extra" Banana switches as backups.

Highly recommend. If you want tactile but not heavy, this is a very affordable option.

reviews.title: Bananas are excellent tactile switch alternatives to browns and blues.
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:dff950435419a7006b0295e50b846c44]] This review is for the Ajazz Diced Fruit Banana Switch.
I wanted to try these switches compared to a brown switch because of its similar characteristics.
These Banana switches are definitely tactile and smooth. They do have a tactile click sound and require a bit more force to actuate (not really noticeable for me because I'm heavy handed and I bottom out the key when I type). I'm not going to mention specs such as actuation force and distance because you can find that info in the product description or manufacturer's site.
I will talk about the "feeling" of these switches though because in everyday use, gaming and office work, I'm not thinking about the actuation force/distance, etc. For me it is how it feels and sounds.

So first off, these are excellent switches, no doubt. It is then just a matter of personal preference whether or not you like the characteristics of the Banana switch. For me, it is a bit too noisy. The tactile bump feels good but after using the Banana's for a while, I would hate typing while on mic because the mic picks up the keystrokes loud and clear and can be annoying for others to hear. I don't have blue switches to compare these Bananas to but I would imagine the blue switches would be worse in sound. I do have red switches to compare and the reds are noticeably quieter, I think mainly due to being linear (no tactile bump). For me and personal preference and usage, I would stick with the reds.

If you like tactile switches and are currently using a different tactile switch, I'd urge you give these Banana switches a try. See my video review where I installed these switches into a keyboard and compare to red switches, so you can get an idea of the sound when the switches are installed into a keyboard frame with keycaps on. In the video I have mistakenly called the red switches Gaterons, but I'm pretty sure they are Kailh reds with box stem.

reviews.title: Nice looking strong tactile
reviews.text:
I love tactile switches, so when this was offered for review I figured I'd give it a shot as I'd heard good things about Ajazz switches. It arrived and I swapped out the switches in my NK65 (DROP Holy Pandas) and threw these into the alphas.

In terms of feel they're a pretty strong tactile. I felt it important to mention that I was using HP's above because these feel very similar to those, just with a lighter spring. The bump is close to identical which is nice, and I prefer the lighter spring as I've been mainlining my Niz lately with 35g switches, so light is my thing right now. In terms of sound, the housing does make these quite a bit higher pitch than HP's, and I'd honestly put it in line with some Akko tactiles. Not too high to not enjoy, but higher than I'm initially used to.

Because these come pre-lubed they can be used stock, which is nice because while once I start I find lubing switches to be relaxing, but it's an activity I dread until I'm actually going. Just something about it seems beyond tedious, and to be fair, it is. Thankfully the lube job on these is pretty good and there's no ping to be heard or scratchiness to be felt. I did lube one just to see the difference that one done by hand can make, and the job on these is good enough that it makes no difference. The switch is fine as-is.

Other than being plate mount only I can't find a problem with these. The spring weight is nice and they sound and feel great to type on. I'm thinking about grabbing another set to build out a board with them in the future.

reviews.title: Kiwi switches
reviews.text:
This is a review for the Kiwi Switch from DAIDAI AJAZZ. The Kiwi switch is tactile, 50gf, and housed in glittery translucent green. If you have clear keycaps, you can see the yellow stem and a little of the upper housing.

For the Kiwi switches, you get 46 switches. For most keyboards, 46 switches is not enough and you will need 2-3 cans.

The switches come in a clear plastic can, with a screw lid and a pull tab. The packaging is cute! More importantly, the can takes up less space than the usual plastic tray, and it’s reusable.

I use Akko Vintage Whites (linear), but I wanted to try tactile switches for the thumb clusters on my ergo keyboard, so I had feedback when I pressed those keys. The tactile bump is smooth and pronounced, I definitely know I pressed that key.

I didn’t notice any difference between these pre-lubed Kiwi switches and non-lubed switches.

reviews.title: Pretty good tactile switches
reviews.text: I've tried linears and even clicky switches, but I really prefer tacticles. These are right in the sweet spot for me in terms of spring weight and tactile bump. Nice colors, if that matters to you. I got these to try out before buying a full set, and I like them quite a bit.

reviews.title: Shine through is very disappointing
reviews.text: I got these primarily because I liked the orange and black color scheme and the advertised shine through features but the backlit brightness is only fair to poor on these (I'm being generous giving it 3 stars, I think).  Even the cheap $6.00 keycap set ($0.38 after discounts) with a similar color scheme I got from aliexpress is brighter than these.  However, since I need those 1u-sized right Alt, FN and Ctrl keycaps for my Royal Kludge RK96 90% keyboard, I'm not returning therm, though I basically paid $20 for those 3 keycaps :/

title: EPOMAKER Airy 143 Keys ESA Profile ANSI ISO Layout Clear Shine Through Keycaps Set for Mechanical Keyboard, Compatible with MX-Clone Switches Cherry/Gateron/Kailh/Akko/Ajazz Switch(Airy Black)
features: 【EPOMAKER Airy Transparent Keycaps】EPOMAKER Airy Keycaps set is designed to fit on a standard ANSI/ISO keyboard with MX style switches. Perhaps you want to give your favorite AZERTY keyboards a makeover to match the modern design trends? Whatever your taste, we’re positive that this is the right choice for you! Shine-through RGB backlight creates an vivid atmosphere which plays an important role in play-ability and mood

【ESA Profile, Compatible with MX Switches】From the community, for the community. EPOMAKER ESA Profile is the best practice of our pursue. ESA profile has a angled and spherical top similar to SA profile. The height is medium but not too tall. It provides unique experience unlike other profiles. This profile is considered as a solid option for both gaming and quick or regular typing

【143 Keys Set, ANSI/ISO Layout Compatible】Apart from the basic full keycaps, this set also contains extra replacement keycaps, including Mac modifiers and ISO special keys, such as big Enter key, 1.25U Shift...Either you are using ANSI standard layout or you are more familiar with ISO layout, we cover it all for you. Simply get one set and meet all needs

【High-Quality Polycabonate Material with UV Printing】Pursuing an ultimate transparency, this set adopts premium PC material to mould the keycaps and printing the characters through digital printing process. The rigorous production ensures the keycaps survive *wear-resistant and scratch-resistant tests. *Note: the test result comes from the laboratory data may differ from actual usage scenes. Due to the material properties, PC keycaps may still be vulnerable to scratches and hand sweat/oil/cream

【Increased Compatibility】EPOMAKER Airy Clear keycaps set contains special shift sizes and modifiers like the 1u, 1.75u and 2u SHIFT key, so it is with more possibilities to customize your EPOMAKER keyboard, such as 60%/65%/75%/TKL/96%/1800-compact/100% keyboard. It also has split spacebar keys that you might need. A keycap set that makes your DIY journey worry-free

reviews.title: Kawaii !!
reviews.text: Comes with all the keys caps you need, love how smooth it feels. You may need to dim your keyboard brightness, found the light through the clear key caps can hurt your eyes after a while.

reviews.title: Exceptionally perfect feel EXCEPT no way to find your position!
reviews.text:
I wanted to love these key caps. They look great! They feel superb and their smooth texture is exactly what I wanted.

However, two major flaws: I didn't even know this was a thing to consider, but they don't have bumps on the positioning keys "F" and "J". So if you type a lot and remove your hands from the keys, you need to look down every time to find your position. This interrupts my workflow as a transcriptionist quite a bit. The other disadvantage is that the smooth feel of the material also makes it impossible to keep skin oil, snack residues, etc. from being left behind on these caps, making them somewhat difficult to grip after the first half hour.

reviews.title: Really nice visual upgrade
reviews.text:
I wanted to upgrade my keyboard’s appearance, and after much Googling I settled on this product. I have a full size 104 key keyboard, and looking at keycaps on various websites makes it seem kinda complicated to know which set I’d need for my specific keyboard (Das Keyboard 4). Some Ctrl keys are wider than others, Spacebars shorter than others, etc. and what I liked about this set is that it includes different length keys and you just use the one that fits your keyboard and ignore the rest.

This is my first set of keycaps so I can’t really speak with any knowledge to the quality of the keys, but I’m really happy with how they look. They feel similar to the ones that came with my keyboard but look muuuuch better.

reviews.title: Fantastic keycap set
reviews.text:
They finally arrived! I plan on putting these keycaps on a 75% keyboard that won't arrive for another couple of months so in the pictures, I only put it on part of my keyboard to test them out. I want to "preserve" that new keycap feeling later. :)

These keycaps are fairly thick and have the standard PBT texture. The doubleshot legends are quite clear and none of the spacebars are warped! This is a new profile I've never typed on and I can't find much information on the web about it so I included a lot of pictures. I compared them in the pictures to my Ducky keyboard with doubleshot ABS. They actually aren't too different from OEM keycap profile (from the Ducky) height but some of the rows are higher. They're much more shapely and the F and J keys have a deeper dish. Compared to DSA keycaps, they don't seem to be quite as deep dishey overall which I actually prefer.
The keycaps fit snug on cherry mx brown stems, more so than my Ducky keycaps. They aren't hard to put on though. Some of the stems have a small part of plastic missing from the MX cross area but it's only a handful of the keycaps and it didn't effect the fit. I've included photos of this as well.
Overall they're great keycaps that I won't be using yet until my keyboard for them arrives.

title: VELOCIFIRE Keycap Puller Tool, 3 in 1 Keycap Puller, Stainless Steel Keycap Removal Tool, Key Pulling, Loosen, Tighten Keycap Stem, Expert for Mechanical Keyboard(Black)
features: This wire style keycap puller is a necessary tool for any mechanical keyboard owner. Easily remove and replace keycaps with this keycap removal tool.
Creative two-stage combination design, multifunctional keycap puller, one part is keycap puller, one part is keycap stem adjust.
You just need to place the keycap puller metal at the bottom of the keycap and pull the keycap out vertically.
Round body shape designed for maximum comfort.Compatible for most mechanical keyboards including Cherry MX，Kailh, Outemu, and Content Switches.
Wires can bend outward to fit onto every key except the spacebar. (The spacebar key can be removed by hand after the surrounding keys are removed).

reviews.title: It's good, but it doesn't fit Daskeyboard keys
reviews.text: I have a das keyboard.  This doesn't fit over the keys.  I had to twist and bend it to make it work.  It's not the right tool for a das keyboard.  With that said, it's a good quality tool.  I would buy it again for something with smaller keys.  Just don't bother for the mechanical das keyboard.

title: LexonElec Custom Coiled Keyboard Cable, 2.0M(0.66ft) USB-C to USB-A TPU Mechanical Keyboard Cable, Detachable Metal Aviator Double-Sleeved Wire for Gaming Keyboard(Orange)
features: 【High Quality and Durable】LexonElec custom coiled keyboard cable Uses four high-quality copper wires and aluminum shielding foil as the core, surface adopts metal and TPU. Professional reverse coil, strong and durable, not afraid of bending and will not break.

【Metal Aviation Connector】The aviator connector allows you to quickly attach different cables to your keyboard or other accessories. Screw-type plug-in lock, self-locking structure, strong connection and stable transmission. All-steel metal, full of texture, Enhance the keyboard experience.
【Professional reversed Coil】Coiled shape, more substantial, not easy to tangle. Even if it is overstretched, it will not only not be damaged but will quickly return to its original shape.

【High-speed Charging and Transfer】USB Type-C to USB-A cable supports safe and fast data synchronization and charging, USB cable is compatible with most USB C mechanical gaming keyboards or printers, PS4, Switch.

【Enough length】The length of the cable exceeds similar products on the market! 7.9 inches long with 20 mm Inner diameter coiled cable+1.1 meter straight cable. This length is enough to meet the distance of all keyboards. It will be a perfect replacement for your OEM or original cables with superior performance.

【Quality Assurance】 After a number of technical tests, including transmission stability, sturdy cables durability, resilience of coils, firm welding of the joints, heavy load test on connect and swing test. Each product you own is high -quality.

reviews.title: Very nice coiled keyboard cable.
reviews.text:
The cable came nicely coiled with no flaws. Plugs are high quality and the screw connection type is pretty heavy duty I don't see this breaking. Only wished the straight part of the cable was longer.

I highly recommend this cable for anyone looking for a custom looking cable that wont break the bank.

reviews.title: Works as advertised!
reviews.text: This cable worked exactly as advertised. My keyboard has a USB c connector, and it fit together perfectly. I love the look of this cable as it neatly tucks under my desk and doesn't leave any unnecessary excess hanging around. Overall very happy with this product!

reviews.title: Decent aviator cable for my keyboard setup.
reviews.text: These cables are an aesthetically great match for my “rebel scum” keyboard build.  The connections are tight and the materials used seems like they are great quality.  I don’t really have any complaints about the quality of this product.  I think it’s important to note these are not braided cables, and have a soft rubber feel, much like you would get on any standard USB type cable.

reviews.title: Good Quality!
reviews.text: Bright white coiled cable makes my desk look neater and organized. Matches my desk decor better. Keyboard response is the same as the original cable it came with, no complaints here.

reviews.title: Treat your keyboard to a nicer cable
reviews.text: What a nice cable, with solid metal USB plugs, a clean aviator connector, and tight and even coils. I got it in purple, to match some keycaps I like, and it looks perfect. The USB-C plug is about 5.5mm thick, so should fit most recessed plugs. I was able to charge every device I tried, even a chromebook (lenovo duet) and phone (pixel 6a).

title: Vaydeer 4 Fully Programmable Keys with Floating Window and Macro Multifunctional Keypad, One-Handed Mechanical Keyboard Support NKRO, Hotkeys, One-Click Start, Compatible with iOS,Windows
features: 🖥 User-Friendly Interface and Floating Window：With user-friendly interface and real-time floating window, you will never forget the function of the key being used at the moment. This one handed macro mechanical keyboard can make your work faster and more efficient, and make the game experience more comfortable and smooth. Besides, you can carry the macro keyboard anywhere due to the compact and elegant design.

🖥 OTA Upgrade and Setting Sharing：The macro keyboard supports OTA online upgrade. Timely push message reminds you to update the firmware for more useful functions. Easy setting and you can export/import your settings for backup. No more set up for different computers. You can also share your settings with friends. If you have any problems with this one-handed macro mechanical keyboard, please feel free to contact us, we are sure to provide you with a satisfactory solution.

reviews.title: Hey, this is nifty
reviews.text:
JUST GOT THIS LAST NIGHT -- CHECK MY PURCHASE DATE -- AND IT WORKS! FEW MINUTES TO UNDERSTAND THE APP AND CONFIGURATION BUT NOW I'M UP AND FLYING.
USING BUTTONS FOR TRANSCRIBING SHORTCUTS; HIGHLIGHTING TEXT, BOLD, UNDERLINE, LARGER, DELETE, PLAY/PAUSE, FORWARD AND REWIND AUDIO/VIDEO TRACKS.
9-KEY *DOES* WORK THE GLOBAL APPLE MEDIA KEYS -- PLAY/PAUSE, REV, FFW AND IT WILL SCRUB AUDIO/VIDEO AS NORMAL AKA HOLDING DOWN THE REWIND KEY IN QUICKTIME, FOR EXAMPLE.
THE DEPRESSING OF KEYS ARE OLD SCHOOL -- LONG THROW AND LOUD -- BUT WILL GET USED TO.
CONTOUR SHUTTLE PRO V2 WILL ALSO GET AN AUDITION.
SUPER HAPPY WITH THIS LITTLE CONTROLLER, IT'S A TIDY SQUARE ON THE TABLE, AS WELL. LONG CABLE LEAD.

reviews.title: Awesome keypad for Day Trading Hotkeys
reviews.text: This keypad is good quality. Keys are tactile and clicky. Board is solid and no flex. Cable is braided and good quality. Very happy with this device. The programming can be flashed to the hardware so you do NOT need to have the software running for this to work.

reviews.title: I live the macro board
reviews.text: I am a software developer and use this everyday. It was easy to program and I love the onscreen UI that tells me what I have mapped to each button.  I also like the ability to store multiple button sets in other “presets” on the board. Overall it works great and I am glad I picked this up.

reviews.title: very good for the price
reviews.text:
I got this for a few different use such as photoshop, gaming, and other work software.
At first, looking at the photo I thought it was a metal case, but it's just a thin metal on the top and everything is plastic. Therefore, it's really light, and when you tap the keys it sounds so hallow, so I end up opening up taping the board, and filling it with some foam. This made a bit better but not much different. As for the software it is easy to use and there are a few layers (profile) settings that you can set it up for different software if needed. Also, the software is very easy to use.

Overall for the price, it is a good macro keypad for many different uses.

reviews.title: Nice Additional Keys
reviews.text: The software couldn’t be any easier to program. Keys have a nice feel to them. You can program up to 6 layers with this software. I use it with my CNC CAD/CAM software for repetitive functions that I use most. I would buy it again.
